sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.76 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$=( _{ij})$,$1 i j n$, be an irreducible elementary net (carpet) of order$n 3$over a field$K$(see 19.48). The net$$is said to be closed if the elementary net subgroup$E( )$does not contain new elementary transvections. The net$$is said to be completable if its diagonal can be supplemented with subgroups to a complete net. Completable elementary nets are closed. It is known that over fields of c...
